name: List
description: A list displays related pieces of information where each item represents only one attribute. It helps organize static content and create visual rhythm, making information easier to scan and understand. Lists are not interactive, so users cannot modify, filter, or otherwise change their contents. If an item needs to show more than one attribute, use a table instead.

table.name: Separator
table.description: Optional line between two list items.

table.name: Marker
table.description: Optional decoration or indicator for the list item.

properties.name: List
properties.description: The grouping of multiple list items.

properties.name: Orientation
properties.description: Whether the list is displayed horizontally or vertically.

properties.name: Text
properties.description: Sets the content of the list

properties.name: Marker
properties.description: Decorator for a single list item.
properties.constraint: All items in the same level of nesting must have the same marker type. No marker allowed when using dots to separate horizontal lists.

properties.name: Divider (vertical list)
properties.description: Whether has muted rule between list items

properties.name: Divider (horizontal list)
properties.description: Whether the list has vertical line as a separator or a dot.

decisionLog.what: The list only displays static text. Links are their own component, so in that case list would be used as a spacing component more than just a simple way to group items. This also opens the door to use the list for any other component, in which rules should probably be considered (groups of cards, buttons, etc).
